# Agenttic

> The evaluation your AI agent can't game. Open-source and on-device: one command
> scores any agent against a rubric fitted to what it actually does, and every
> score traces to a check you can audit. Not a leaderboard — a measurement
> instrument. MIT-licensed.

Agenttic runs entirely on your hardware. Your agent, prompts, and traces never
leave your machine; the only calls that leave are the ones your own judge and
generator steps make to the model provider you configure, under your own keys
(point them at a local model like Ollama to stay fully on-device). No telemetry.

## How it works

Install → fit → prove. Agenttic installs as a skill in the assistant you already
use (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Codex, Gemini CLI, Aider) and as an MCP server.

```
uv tool install agenttic
agenttic install            # add the skill
agenttic eval ./my-agent    # or /agenttic eval inside your assistant
```

One run classifies your agent into an archetype and fits a rubric to it — proven
criteria for that kind of agent plus the specifics of yours — then refuses to ship
that rubric until it proves the rubric separates good agents from bad. Your agent
runs the fitted suite and every criterion is scored with its evidence attached.

## The verdict, not a number

Every criterion is tagged with how it was measured:

- deterministic — a code check on the trace; same input, same result, no model in
  the loop.
- judged · calibrated (α) — an LLM judge that agrees with human reviewers at a
  measured agreement α against a known human ceiling.
- provisional — scored by a judge not yet calibrated on this criterion; shown,
  flagged, never counted as certain.

A judge is never allowed to be more confident than the humans it is measured
against.

## Why a rubric, not a benchmark

- Fit: a rubric fitted to the agent's archetype and proven to discriminate, not
  one fixed test for all agents.
- Provenance: every score traces to a check, a trace, and a rationale you can open
  — not an opaque aggregate.
- Reliability: pass^k measures consistency across k independent runs, not a lucky
  single run. The gap between pass^1 and pass^8 is the flakiness number, named in
  every report.
- Contamination: private suites with per-tenant canaries, not public repos that
  models were likely trained on.

## Pricing

The core is free and open source under the MIT license: the harness, the
deterministic checks, the LLM-judge mechanism (bring your own key), the reports,
and the MCP server. No account, no card. A separate early-access layer — the
rubric engine, the improvement loop, calibration at scale, and neutral
certification — is Agenttic Assurance, for teams.

## Links

- Methodology: https://agenttic.io/methodology
- Pricing: https://agenttic.io/pricing
- Scan an agent: https://agenttic.io/scan
- System status: https://agenttic.io/status
- Verify a certificate: https://agenttic.io/certified
